notes.transitive_depth_truncation: The lockfile includes direct deps of request@2.88.2 and the first transitive level. Deeper transitives (e.g. sshpk's deps: asn1, jsbn, getpass, dashdash, ecc-jsbn, tweetnacl, bcrypt-pbkdf, safer-buffer; jsprim's deps: extsprintf, json-schema, verror; ajv's deps: uri-js, fast-deep-equal, json-schema-traverse, fast-json-stable-stringify) are omitted from both the lockfile and this expected-tree for brevity. A real `bun install` would resolve them. The comparator should check the probe's direct deps and first-level transitives; deeper transitives are documented in warnings[] but not asserted here.
notes.deprecation_status: request@2.88.2 is deprecated (not unpublished). The tarball and manifest are accessible from the npm registry. The npm manifest carries `deprecated: 'request has been deprecated, see https://github.com/request/request/issues/3142'`. This is the documented sub-case of E1 (unpublished-version): the lockfile-pinned version is no longer maintained and carries a registry-level deprecation flag.
notes.exact_pin_rationale: The constraint is declared as `'2.88.2'` (exact, no operator) rather than `'^2.88.2'` or `'~2.88.2'`. This makes version-substitution detectable: if Mend silently resolves to a higher non-deprecated version, the diff is unambiguous.

warnings: E1 (unpublished-version): request@2.88.2 is DEPRECATED — the npm registry manifest includes a `deprecated` field with message: 'request has been deprecated, see https://github.com/request/request/issues/3142'. The package was deprecated on 2020-02-11. It is NOT unpublished (tarball remains accessible), but the deprecation flag is present in every registry manifest fetch for this version.

EXPECTED MEND BEHAVIOR: Mend should report request@2.88.2 AS-PINNED in the lockfile. The declared constraint is the exact version '2.88.2'. The deprecation flag is metadata on the registry response — it must not alter the resolved version, drop the dep, or trigger a fallback to a different version.

FAILURE MODE 1 — version substitution: Mend silently resolves request to a nearby non-deprecated version (e.g. the latest non-deprecated 2.x or some heuristic 'safe' version). Detection: reported version does not match '2.88.2'. This is the most common failure class for deprecated packages.

FAILURE MODE 2 — dep dropped entirely: Mend treats the `deprecated` flag as a signal to exclude the package from the tree (false negative). Detection: request is absent from the reported dep tree entirely. Any downstream transitives (qs, aws4, uuid, etc.) may also be missing.

FAILURE MODE 3 — stale cache hit: Mend's registry manifest cache was populated from an earlier non-deprecated manifest snapshot. The cache entry may carry different dependency declarations or a different version resolution. Detection: transitives reported under request do not match the constraint-resolved versions above (e.g. qs@6.5.2 instead of 6.5.3, or form-data@2.3.2 instead of 2.3.3).

FAILURE MODE 4 — stale vulnerability data: Mend reports vulnerability data derived from the deprecated version's manifest, which may itself be out of date relative to the canonical version history. This is a data-quality failure, not a tree-shape failure. Detection: vulnerability scan findings for request@2.88.2 omit CVEs that were added to the advisory database after the deprecation date.

FAILURE MODE 5 — deprecation flag as dep-type reclassification: Mend changes the dep group from 'main' to some internal 'deprecated' or 'excluded' category, causing the dep to be omitted from standard vulnerability scanning. Detection: request absent from the vulnerability scan results even though it appears in the dep tree.

LOCKFILE FORMAT: bun.lock text JSONC (Bun 1.1+, lockfileVersion 1). Mend detection is lockfile-driven only — no install step. If Mend cannot parse bun.lock JSONC (e.g. due to trailing commas or JSONC comment syntax), the entire tree will be empty. This is a parser prerequisite, not an E1-specific failure, but it must be confirmed before asserting on E1 behavior.

Bun is NOT in the Mend install-tool list. Detection is lockfile-driven only (bun.lock JSONC parsing). scanSettings.versioning cannot pin a Bun toolchain. No .whitesource file is emitted with this probe.

TRANSITIVE TRUNCATION: The lockfile and this expected-tree include direct deps of request@2.88.2 and the first transitive level. Deeper transitives (sshpk's 9 deps, jsprim's 4 deps, ajv's 4 deps) exist in reality but are omitted from this probe for tractability. A production comparator should use `gte` tolerance for total dep counts.

PAIRED WITH probe #24 (bun-not-in-install-tool-probe): that probe documents the install-tool limitation independently. This probe exercises E1 behavior assuming the lockfile parser functions correctly.

REGISTRY EVIDENCE: The deprecation of request can be verified at https://registry.npmjs.org/request/2.88.2 (field `deprecated`) and at https://github.com/request/request/issues/3142 (the upstream retirement announcement from 2020-02-11).
